title: Fix stale sanitizer source-contract tests on upstream-backport-bulk
description: Sanitizer matrix run 20260622T221537Z failed across all lanes because a cluster of source-contract tests appear pinned to outdated literals or source shapes rather than because of a first user-code sanitizer defect. Investigate the failing tests, confirm the live source-of-truth behavior, update the tests or code narrowly, and rerun the focused repros plus the affected lane as needed.

title: Audit PR23 batch 8 business and auth backports
description: Audit docs/Plans/UPSTREAM_BACKPORT_PR23_AUDIT_PLAN_2026-06-21.md batch 8 against repo architecture, OWASP ASVS L2, fail-closed auth/business invariants, and regression coverage. If a defect is proven, add red-first contract/adversarial/integration coverage, fix minimally, and verify the targeted slice.
close_reason: Completed batch-8 audit, fixed web-token auth state poisoning and BotAccessSettings invalid-payload null-deref, verified batch-8 guard pack.

title: Fix web-token auth 2FA fallback and DC pinning in AuthManager
description: Batch 8 PR23 audit found that checkAuthenticationWebToken sets transient auth state correctly, but SESSION_PASSWORD_NEEDED fallback still excludes ImportWebTokenAuthorization and the follow-up password fetch/continuation path is only pinned for ImportQrCode. This can fail closed incorrectly or continue 2FA on the wrong DC for web-token logins. Add red-first contract, adversarial, and integration tests; apply the minimal AuthManager fix; rerun the batch-8 auth/business slices.

title: Audit PR23 batch 8 auth/business backport invariants
description: Audit the staged PR23 batch-8 AuthManager and BusinessConnectedBot changes against fail-closed auth state handling, business serialization correctness, and regression coverage. If a real defect is proven, add red-first tests in separate files, apply the minimal fix, and rerun the batch-8 slice to green.
notes: Batch-8 audit completed. Proven defect: import_web_token_authorization now marks was_web_token_login_request_=true, but reset_wait_phone_number_query_state previously failed to clear the same transient flag on WaitPhoneNumber error handling. Added red-first contract/adversarial/integration tests for web-token auth state, fixed the reset path minimally, and verified the targeted slices. BusinessConnectedBot location serialization regression tests are also green. Separate follow-up filed as tdlib-obf-0pc for unrelated ManagedBotAccessLayerContract failure seen only under a broad substring filter.

title: Audit PR23 batch 2 verification gaps
description: Audit and tighten the PR23 batch 2 proof for ManagedBotCreated and call-notification backports. Current plan filter command selects zero tests, and the call-notification proof does not fully pin the call-site state mutation and helper argument contract. Add stricter tests first, keep changes scoped to the batch-2 audit surface, rerun the correct targeted filters, and record findings.

title: Build comprehensive end-to-end backport validation pipeline
description:
Why this exists and what needs to be done:
The current repository has real end-to-end harness entry points in test/tdclient.cpp and test/online.cpp, but they are too auth/network-heavy and not productized as a reusable validation pipeline for backport audits. As a result, parser/repair/helper backport reviews often rely on seam tests and source contracts instead of deterministic full actor/database coverage, which lowers confidence and leaves parts of the change surface effectively blind.

Why this must be tracked in Beads:
Other agents working this GitHub repository need a durable, shared reminder that we should extend the existing harness into a comprehensive E2E testing pipeline. The goal is to stop auditing backport changes blindly and to provide a reproducible actor/database validation path that future agents can invoke and extend.

What needs to be done:
Design and implement a comprehensive E2E testing pipeline built on the existing harnesses. It should support deterministic database reopen flows, actor lifecycle coverage, targeted reproduction paths for backported parser/repair/runtime changes, and a practical way to validate high-risk backports without requiring ad hoc live authorization flows. The outcome should be a documented, reusable audit path that complements seam/runtime tests rather than replacing them.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Document the intended E2E pipeline architecture and when to use it versus seam/runtime tests.
2. Add at least one reusable fixture or driver layer over the existing tdclient/online harnesses for deterministic backport validation.
3. Cover at least one database reload/reopen scenario and one actor-lifecycle scenario through the new pipeline.
4. Wire the pipeline into repo test docs so future audits can invoke it consistently.
5. Ensure focused validation commands are practical enough to use during backport review work.
notes: This task exists because current backport review coverage is still partially blind without a reusable full actor/database E2E pipeline. The repo already has tdclient.cpp and online.cpp, but they are not enough as a documented, deterministic audit pipeline.

title: Harden PR23 backport audit coverage on adaptive-runtime-profile-rotation
description: Audit follow-up for focal upstream-backport commits on feat/adaptive-runtime-profile-rotation. Replace source-string contract checks with behavior-sensitive TDD coverage where practical, verify whether any focal backports hide real correctness regressions, and only patch production code if a red test proves a defect.

title: Investigate failing ManagedBotAccessLayerContract on current branch
description: During the PR23 batch-8 audit, the broad test filter ./build/test/run_all_tests --filter BotAccessSettings selected Test_ManagedBotAccessLayerContract_LayerMustBe225ForManagedBotAccessSettingsConstructors and it failed immediately at test/managed_bot_access_layer_contract.cpp:26. This appears unrelated to the staged AuthManager/BusinessConnectedBot delta but should be triaged separately so broad managed-bot access verification is green again.
